provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Councilors' Election (Registration of Voters) Regulations, 2004.

provisions.title: Application and effective date
provisions.content: These Regulations shall apply for the registration of voters for councilors' election and shall come into force on the date of publication in the Gazette .

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires:— " Act " means the Local Authorities (Elections) Act; " Constitution " means the Constitution of the United Republic of Tanzania of 1977; [ Act No. 4 of 1979 ] " Director of Elections " means the Director of Elections provided for under the Article 74(7) of the Constitution and appointed under section 6 of the Elections Act , 1985 and includes a person for the time being performing any of the functions on his behalf; [ Act No. 1 of 1985 ] " election " means an election in a ward of an elected councilor and includes a by-election for that purpose. " election officer " includes a public officer, a temporary employee and an employee on contract terms working with the Electoral Authority ; " Electoral Authority " means the National Electoral Commission established under Article 74 of the Constitution ; " Political Party " means a political party registered under the Political Parties Act , 1992; [ Act No. 5 of 1992 ] " Provisional Voters' Register " means a register established under section 15 A of the Act ; " register " means a, Permanent National Voters' Register compiled and maintained in accordance with section 15B of the Act ; " registration centre " means a voters registration centre in a ward ; " registration record " means voter's registration record compiled in accordance with the provisions of the Act and these Regulations. " Registration Staff " includes the a Registration Officer, an Assistant Registration Officer, a Registration Assistant and a Camera Operator; " ward " means a division of the jurisdiction of a local government authority, demarcated as such, in pursuance of the provisions of the Act and declared to be a ward for the purpose of election under section 8 of the Act ; " voter's card " means a card issued under the Act , verifying that a person named in such card has been registered as voter. Part II – Appointment of registration staff

provisions.title: Registration Officers
provisions.content: (1) There shall be Registration Officers to conduct, coordinate and supervise registration of voters in wards. (2) The Registration Officers under sub-regulation (1) shall be as provided for under section 10(1) of the Act . (3) The Electoral Authority shall at any time before registration of voters, inform Registration Officers the following: (a) the date of commencement of their duty under sub-regulation (1) ; (b) terms and conditions of their duty; (c) the procedure and manner in which registration of voters shall be conducted; and (d) any other directions as may be determined by the Electoral Authority or the Director of Elections .

provisions.title: Appointment of Registration Officers by the Commission
provisions.content: (1) Notwithstanding regulation 4(2) , the Electoral Authority may where it is satisfied that the Registration Officer provided for under section 10 (1) the Act , is for whatever reason incapable of performing his duty: (a) appoint any public officer to be a Registration Officer; and (b) inform the Registration Officer provided for under the Act accordingly. (2) The letter of appointment for the Registration Officer under sub-regulation (1) , shall contain: (a) the date of commencement of duty and tenure of office; (b) terms and conditions of his duty, (c) the procedure and manner in which registration of voters shall be conducted. (d) any other directions as may be determined by the Electoral Authority or the Director of Elections . (3) The appointed Registration Officer shall, within fourteen days after receiving the letter of appointment, informs the Electoral Authority in writing of acceptance of the appointment. (4) Where the appointed Registration Officer without good cause fails to comply with sub-regulation (3) , the Electoral Authority shall appoint another public officer to be a Registration Officer.

provisions.title: Appointment of Assistant Registration Officers
provisions.content: (1) The Electoral Authority shall, subject to section 10(2) of the Act , appoint in writing Assistant Registration Officers from amongst public officers. (2) The letter of appointment for the Assistant Registration Officer under sub-regulation (1) shall contain:— (a) the date of commencement of duty; (b) tenure of office; (c) the terms and conditions of his duty; and (d) any other directions as may be determined by the Electoral Authority or the Director of Elections . (3) The appointed Assistant Registration Officer shall, within fourteen days after receiving the letter of appointment, inform the Electoral Authority in writing of acceptance of the appointment. (4) Where the appointed Assistant Registration Officer without good cause fails to comply with sub-regulation (3) , the Electoral Authority shall appoint another public officer to be the Assistant Registration Officer. (5) The Assistant Registration Officer appointed under this Regulation, shall perform his duties under the supervision of the Registration Officer.

provisions.title: Appointment of Registration Assistants, Camera Operators and other staff
provisions.content: (1) Every’ Registration Officer shall, subject to the provisions of the Act , these Regulations and directions issued by the Electoral Authority or the Director of Elections , appoint in writing, a Registration Assistant. Camera Operator or any other staff for every registration centre . (2) The Electoral Authority shall determine the number of Registration Assistants or Camera Operators required for every Registration Centre. (3) Any person appointed under this regulation shall be required to sign a contract of employment with the Registration Officer. (4) Subject to the provisions of the Act , these Regulations and Directions issued by the Electoral Authority or Director of Elections , every Registration Assistant and a Camera Operator or other staff appointed under sub-regulation (1) shall perform his duties under the supervision of the Registration Officer or an Assistant Registration Officer, as the case may be.

provisions.title: Procedure for appointment of Registration Assistants and a Camera Operators
provisions.content: (1) The Registration Officer shall, in appointing Registration Assistants and Camera Operators: (a) advertise posts inviting qualified persons to apply; (b) display names of applicants at the Office of the Registration Officer; or any other public office in the ward ; (c) interview the applicants; (2) The Registration Officer shall immediately after conducting an interview under sub-regulation (1) , appoint successful applicant as Registration Assistant or as the case may be a Camera Operator

provisions.title: Oaths of secrecy and seclarations
provisions.content: Every Registration Officer and Assistant Registration Officer shall, before assuming their duties: (a) take an oath of secrecy in Form No. 6 prescribed in the Schedule; and (b) make a declaration before a Magistrate or Commissioner for Oaths that, he is not a member of any political party or that he has withdrawn his membership from a Political Party in Form No. 7 prescribed in the Schedule.

provisions.title: Training of registration staff
provisions.content: The Electoral Authority shall, at any time after the appointment of Registration staff and before commencement of registration of voters, arrange and facilitate training to Registration Staff on the registration process, procedure and law. Part III – Registration of voters A – Applications for registration, and Provisional Voters' Register

provisions.title: Period for general registration of voters
provisions.content: (1) A general registration of voters shall be carried out under these Regulations, in registration centres within all wards at such intervals as the Electoral Authority shall by Notice published in the Gazette determine, and such registration may be carried out in different wards or councils at different times as the Electoral Authority may deem fit. (2) Subject to the provisions of the Act and these Regulations, every Registration Officer shall, establish registration centre in a ward for the purposes of registration of voters.

provisions.title: Extension of registration period
provisions.content: The Electoral Authority may, where it appears necessary, extend the registration period specified under sub-regulation (1) of regulation 11 , in respect of any ward .

provisions.title: Registration of voters
provisions.content: Every Registration Assistant shall, subject to the provisions of the Act , these Regulations, and Directions issued by the Electoral Authority or the Director of Elections , register voters at a registration centre within every ward .

provisions.title: Application for registration
provisions.content: (1) Any person who qualifies to be registered as a voter may, make an application to the Registration Assistant to be registered as a voter within the registration period specified for that ward . (2) Where the Registration Assistant to whom the application is made under sub-regulation (1) , is satisfied that the applicant qualifies to be registered as a voter, he shall, cause the applicant to be photographed and registered in Form No. 1. (3) Form No. 1 shall be as prescribed in the schedule to these Regulations and shall contain following: (a) particulars of the applicant/voter stating: (a) first name and two other names, as recorded under sub-regulation 2 of regulation 14 ; (b) gender of the applicant; (c) date of birth; (d) residential address; (e) occupation;. (f) place of birth; (g) citizenship; (h) registration centre ; (i) whether or not the applicant is disabled; (j) signature and thumbprint; and (k) date of registration; and (b) name and signature of the Registration Assistant. (4) Every Form No. 1 shall in addition to particulars under sub-regulation (3) be attached with a passport-size photograph.

provisions.title: Voter's card
provisions.content: (1) The Registration Assistant shall, immediately after registering the applicant as a voter under sub-regulation (2) of regulation 14 , issue to the applicant a voter's card . (2) A voter's card issued to the applicant under sub-regulation (1) shall be as prescribed in the Schedule and it shall contain: (a) particulars of a voter:— (i) first name and two other names, as recorded under sub-regulation 2 of regulation 14 ; (ii) gender of the applicant; (ii) number of registration; [Please note: numbering as in the original.] (iv) date of birth; (v) place of birth; (vi) residential address; (vii) registration centre ; and (viii) signature or thumbprint; (b) signature of the Registration Assistant. (2) The voter's card issued under sub-regulation (2) , shall in addition to the specified particulars be attached with a passport-size photograph.

provisions.title: Refusal to register
provisions.content: (1) The Registration Assistant may, if not satisfied that the applicant qualifies for registration under regulation 14 , refuse to register the applicant as a voter. (2) Where a Registration Assistant has refused to register any person as a voter, he shall, if so required by such person, notify him the grounds for refusal, in a manner set out in Form No. 3 prescribed in the Schedule to these Regulations. (3) Any applicant who is aggrieved by refusal of his application under this regulation, may, within seven days from the date of notification under sub-regulation (2) , forward the matter together with Form No. 3 to the Registration Officer who shall determine whether the applicant qualifies to be registered as a voter and notify the applicant accordingly. (4) Any applicant who is aggrieved by the decision of the Registration Officer may, within fourteen days after receiving the decision of the Registration Officer, appeal against such decision to the District Court. (5) The District Court shall determine such appeal within fourteen days from the date of submission of appeal.

provisions.title: Submission of registration record and forms to Registration Officer
provisions.content: (1) The Registration Assistant shall, after registration period specified in respect of any ward under regulation 11 , submit to the Registration Officer or Assistant Registration Officer, registration forms and data which shall include— (a) each voter's registration particulars in Form No. 1 prescribed in the Schedule; (b) the daily number of registered voters in Form No. 2 prescribed in the Schedule; (c) the unused and spoilt Forms No. 1 No. 2; (d) Form No. 3 prescribed in the Schedule; and (e) any other record or information as may be directed by the Director of Elections . (2) Where the Assistant Registration Officer has received the registration record and forms as required under sub-regulation (1) , he shall as soon as practicable submit or cause them to be submitted to the Registration Officer. (3) The Registration Officer shall, forward to the Director of Elections , the registration records and registration Forms under sub-regulation (1) for every registration centre as submitted to the Registration Officer by the Registration Assistant under sub-regulation (1) , or as the case may be, the Assistant Registration Officer under sub regulation (2).

provisions.title: Provisional Voters Register
provisions.content: (1) The Director of Elections shall, after receiving voters' registration record and Forms in respect of the registration centres for every ward under regulation 17 , cause to be prepared a Provisional Voters' Register for every ward . (2) The Provisional Voters' Register shall be in such form as the Commission may determine.

provisions.title: Particulars in the Provisional Voters' Register
provisions.content: Subject to regulation 18 , the Provisional Voters' Register shall contain for each voter the following:— (a) first name and two other names, if any as recorded under sub-regulation 3 of regulation 14 ; (b) gender of the applicant; (c) date of birth; (d) registration number; and (e) any other particulars as the Electoral Authority may determine.

provisions.title: Delivery of Provisional Voters' Register to Registration Officers
provisions.content: (1) After a Provisional Voters' Register is prepared in respect of every ward under regulation 18 , the Director of Elections shall cause to be delivered to the Registration Officers, such number of copies of the Provisional Voters' Register as the Electoral Authority may determine.

provisions.title: Display of Provisional Voters' Register
provisions.content: Subject to the Act , these Regulations, and Directions issued by the Director of Elections or the Electoral Authority , a Registration Officer shall display or cause to be displayed the Provisional Voters' Register for every ward for inspection by the public during office hours for a period not exceeding seven days or such other period as may be determined by the Electoral Authority . B – Objections, claims and appeals

provisions.title: Objection on voter registration and procedure and determination
provisions.content: (1) The following shall have the right to object to registration of a voter in any ward : (a) any person whose name appears in the Provisional Voters' Register of a ward ; (b) the Registration Officer or Assistant Registration Officer; (c) the Director of Elections . (2) Subject to the provisions of the Act , objection against registration of any voter may be lodged with the Registration Officer within seven days from the last date of the display of the Provisional Voters' Register . (3) Every objection shall be made in duplicate in form No. 4 prescribed in the Schedule. (4) The Registration Officer after receiving an objection, shall: (a) Within seven days from the date of receiving the objection, inform the objected person the grounds of objection and require such person to give a written explanation; (b) issue a seven days notice specifying the place, date and time conducting a public inquiry; (c) conduct a public inquiry on the objection; and (d) determine the objection and inform the parties accordingly.

provisions.title: Appeals
provisions.content: (1) Any person who is aggrieved by the decision of the Registration Officer under regulation 22 may, within fourteen days from the date of such decision, appeal to the District Court where a ward is situated. (2) The District Court shall determine the appeal within fourteen days from the date of submission of appeal and forward to the Registration Officer a statement containing name or names to be deleted from the register or name of a person to whom a voters' card shall be issued. (3) Subject to sub-regulation (2) , the Registration Officer shall cause:— the Provisional Voters' Register to be amended, issue or cancel a voter's card , as the case may be, and inform the Director of Elections accordingly.

provisions.title: Times for making changes in the Provisional Voters Register
provisions.content: The Electoral Authority shall specify in the Gazette , or in any other appropriate manner the period, time and the deadline for making any change in the Provisional Voters' Register in respect of: (a) issuing another voter's card to replace a lost, defaced or destroyed voter's card ; (b) transferring particulars of a voter to another ward ; (c) amending particulars of a voter; (d) entering the name of a voter who is inadvertently omitted or not included in the Provisional Voters' Register ; (e) deleting the name of a dead or disqualified voter in Form No. 5B as prescribed in the Schedule; and (f) any other change which the Electoral Authority may deem necessary.

provisions.title: Replacement of a lost or defaced voters' card
provisions.content: (1) Where a voter's card issued to a person is lost, defaced or destroyed, that person may report the matter to the Registration Officer. (2) Subject to sub-regulation (1) , where a Registration Officer is satisfied as to the circumstances of loss, defacement or destruction of a voter's card , he shall: (a) require such person to make a statutory declaration in Form No. 5A prescribed in the Schedule; (b) enter particulars of such voter in Form No. 5A prescribed in the Schedule and issue another voter's card ; (c) complete Form No. 2, prescribed in the Schedule; and (d) forward all the Forms and the registration record to the Director of Elections .

provisions.title: Transfer of registration particulars
provisions.content: (1) Subject to regulation 24 , a person registered as voter in any ward who becomes ordinarily resident in another ward , may apply to the Registration Officer to be registered as a voter in that ward . (2) Any Registration Officer to whom the application is made under this regulation, shall satisfy himself that the applicant ordinarily resides in the ward in which the application is made and determine whether the applicant qualifies to be registered as a voter. (3) The applicant shall subject to sub-regulation (2) , surrender the voter's card to the Registration Officer of the ward to which the voter is ordinarily resident. (4) Subject to sub-regulations (1) , (2) and (3) , the Registration Officer shall register the applicant in Form No. 5A and issue to the applicant another voter's card . (5) Where a Registration Officer registers an applicant under this regulation, the Registration Officer shall: (a) complete Form No. 2; and (b) submit to the Director of Elections completed Form No. 5A and Form No. 2 together with surrendered voter's card if any.

provisions.title: Amendment of particulars
provisions.content: (1) Subject to sub-regulation (2) , where any of the particulars on the voter's card or in a Provisional Voters' Register requires to be amended by reason of change of name or of any alteration in circumstances affecting the person to whom a voter's card was issued, such person may apply to the Registration Officer for another voter's card . (2) Where the Registration Officer is satisfied that the applicant is qualified for registration, he shall: (a) require such person to surrender his voter's card ; (b) register the applicant in Form No. 5A prescribed in the Schedule and issue to the applicant another voter's card . (3) Where the applicant is unable to surrender a voter's card under sub-regulation (2) of regulation 26 , on the ground that such voter's card is lost, defaced or destroyed, the applicant shall make a statutory declaration as provided under regulation 25 . (4) Where a Registration Officer registers an applicant under this regulation, the Registration Officer shall: (a) complete Form No. 2; (b) submit to the Director of Elections completed Forms No. 5A, No. 2 together with the surrendered voter's card if any.

provisions.title: Omitted names in the register
provisions.content: (1) Subject to regulation 24 , any person who is registered as a voter under regulation 14 , and such voter's name does not appear in the Provisional Voters' Register , may apply to the Registration Officer to be included in the Provisional Voters' Register . (2) The Registration Officer shall determine the application under sub-regulation (1) within seven days from the date of receiving the application. (3) Where a Registration Officer determines any application under this regulation that the applicant was registered as a voter in the ward and that the applicant's name is omitted from the Provisional Voter's Register for that ward , he shall: (a) require the applicant to surrender the voters' card and register : register the applicant under Form No. 5A and complete Form No. 2; (b) Submit completed Forms No. 5A, No. 2 and surrendered voter's card , if any to the Director of Elections .

provisions.title: Delivery of registration record to the Director of Elections
provisions.content: The Registration Officer shall, after effecting changes in the Provisional Voter's Register pursuant to regulations 23 , 24 , 25 , 26 , 27 and 28 in respect of a ward , deliver to the Director of Elections the following: (a) a copy of determination to every objection submitted to the Registration Officer under regulation 22 ; (b) a copy of statement of the District Court issued to the Registration Officer in respect of appeals against registration of any voter under regulation 23 : (c) voter's registration record completed by the Registration Officer under regulations 23 , 25 , 26 , 27 and 28 together with the surrendered voter's cards, if any; (d) a copy of determination to an application under regulation 28(3) : and (e) any other information as may be directed by Commission. C – Register of voters and certification

provisions.title: Preparation of voter’s register
provisions.content: (1) Subject to regulation 29 , the Director of Elections shall, as soon as practicable, cause to be prepared a register of voters for every ward . (2) A register of voters prepared under this regulation, shall contain the following particulars for each voter: (a) first name and two other names, as recorded under Sub-regulation (2) of regulation 14 : (b) gender of the voter; (c) date of birth; (d) registration number; (e) a passport - size photograph; and (f) any other particulars as the Electoral Authority as the Electoral Authority may determine. (3) Subject to sub-regulation (2) , the particulars entered in a Provisional Voters Register under regulation 19 for any ward shall be entered in a register of voters for that ward prepared under this regulation. (4) No entry shall be made in a register of voters prepared under this regulation, in respect of any person who has not surrendered a voter's card as required under regulations 23 , 26 , 27 and 28 . (5) The particulars described in regulation 19 shall be in a register of voters prepared for any ward under this regulation, in respect of: (a) any person registered in such ward under regulation 14 ; (b) any person whose name is in the opinion of the Director of Elections , omitted from the Provisional Voters' Register for such a ward ; (c) any person whom the registration officer for such a ward is required to register under regulation 23 , in consequence of an appeal; (d) any person whose application to be included in the Provisional Voters Register is accepted under regulation 28 .

provisions.title: Certification of the register of voters
provisions.content: (1) When the preparation of any register of voters for any ward is completed under regulation 30 , the Director of Elections shall certify such register of voters as the register of voters for that ward . (2) Upon certification of a register of voters under this regulation, such register of voters shall have effect in respect of the ward to which it relates, and any other register of voters prepared in respect of that ward prior to such certification shall thereupon cease to have effect. (3) When the certification of the register of voters for any ward is completed under this regulation, the Director of Elections shall publish a notice in the Gazette stating that:— (a) the certification of the register of voters for such ward has been so completed; and (b) such certified register of voters replaces all registers of voters previously prepared in respect of such a ward .

provisions.title: Delivery of registers after certification
provisions.content: The Director of Elections shall, after certification of register of voters under regulation 31 , deliver to the Registration Officers such number of copies of registers as the Electoral Authority may determine.

provisions.title: Replacement, control, and custody of Provisional Voter's Register and certified register of voters
provisions.content: (1) Every Provisional Voters' Register , prepared under these Regulations, shall, subject to the provisions of this Part, be kept under the control and custody of the Director of Elections and shall not, save for the purposes of these Regulations, be removed from such custody. (2) Every register of voters prepared and certified under this Part, shall be kept under the control and custody of the Director of Elections and shall not, save with the consent of the Electoral Authority or by order of court of competent jurisdiction, be removed from such custody.

provisions.title: Other registration procedures and forms
provisions.content: The Electoral Authority , election officers and registration staff shall, besides these Regulations, observe and comply with the provisions of the Constitution , Act and Directions issued by the Electoral Authority for the purpose of registration of voters which are not covered under these Regulations. Part IV – Miscellaneous

provisions.title: Insertions, corrections and alterations in the register
provisions.content: (1) The Director of Elections may, at any time after the certification of any register of voters under these Regulations, make such insertions, alterations and amendments in the register as may be reasonably required to:— (a) correct any clerical errors; (b) correct any misnomer or incorrect description; (c) ensure that the name of any person does not appear in more than one register or in the same register of voters more than once. (2) Where the Director of Elections makes any insertion, alteration or amendment in a register of voters under this regulation, he shall cause such insertion, alteration or amendment to be made in every copy of the register of voters.

provisions.title: Surrendering a voter’s card of a dead person
provisions.content: (1) Where any person to whom a voter's card is issued dies, any person who is in possession of such voter’s card, shall as soon as practicable, surrender such voter's card to a Registration Officer. (2) A Registration Officer to whom a voter's card has been surrendered under sub-regulation (1) , shall make such inquiries as may be necessary to satisfy himself that the person to whom the voter's card was issued is indeed dead. (3) Where, pursuant sub-regulation (2) , a Registration Officer is satisfied that the person whose name appears on the voter's card is dead, he shall complete Form No. 5B and Form No. 2 and forward to the Director of Elections the Forms and the surrendered voter's card , if any.

provisions.title: Director of Elections and Registration Officers may administer oaths
provisions.content: Any Registration Officer or the Director of Elections may, for the purpose of considering or determining any matter which he is required to consider or determine under these Regulations or of satisfying himself as to the necessity of making any insertion, alteration or amendment in any register of voters, summon any person to appear before him to give evidence on oath or affirmation, and may administer oaths or affirmations for such purpose and may order the production of any document bearing on any issue relevant thereto.

provisions.title: Publication of notice
provisions.content: Where any notice is required by these Regulations to be published and, in the opinion of the authority which is required to publish the notice: the prescribed mode of publication does not give sufficient publicity to the notice, he may, in addition to publishing the notice as required by these Regulations, exhibit copies of the notice in prominent places within the polling district to which the notice relates, or take such other steps as he may deem necessary for giving sufficient publicity thereto.

provisions.title: Voter's card deemed to be cancelled
provisions.content: Any voter's card required to be surrendered under these Regulations; or which is lost, defaced or destroyed, shall be deemed to be cancelled. Part V – Offences

provisions.title: Offences in relation to registration
provisions.content: Any person who, knowing that he is not qualified for registration as a voter, makes an application to be registered under regulation 14 commits an offence.

provisions.title: Offences relating to voter's card
provisions.content: Any person who: (a) being required to surrender a voter's card under these Regulations, willfully fails to do so; (b) without lawful authority, is in possession of a voter's card which was not given to him in pursuance of the Act or these Regulations; or (c) without lawful authority, asks or requires any other person to give to him a voters card given to such other person under these Regulations; commits an offence.

provisions.title: Offences by registration staff or an election officer
provisions.content: A registration staff or an election officer who: (a) willfully fails to perform the functions of his office under these Regulations; (b) fraudulently completes, alters or destroys voters registration record or a voters card; or (c) gives to a person a voter's card otherwise than in pursuance of these Regulations; commits an offence.

provisions.title: False information
provisions.content: Any person who knowingly gives false information to registration staff; commits an offence.

provisions.title: Forgery falsification and fraudulent destruction
provisions.content: Any person who forges, or falsifies, or fraudulently destroys (a) registration record ; (b) a voter's card ; (c) a Register of Voters'; or (d) a Provisional Voters' Register ; commits an offence.

provisions.title: Possession of forged or false documents
provisions.content: Any person who without lawful excuse is in possession of any registration record or a voter's card which is forged or is false; commits an offence.

provisions.title: False entries
provisions.content: Any, applicant, objector or appellant who knowingly makes any false entry in a form completed by such applicant, objection or appeal, appellant in pursuance of these Regulations for the purpose of his application, objection, an appeal or a declaration, as the case may be; commits an offence.

provisions.title: Refusal to leave premises
provisions.content: Any person who fails to leave any premises being used by a registration staff or election officer for the purposes of registering persons as voters or related matters under these Regulations, upon being lawfully required to leave such premises by such registration staff or election officer ; commits an offence.

provisions.title: Penalty
provisions.content: Any person who commits an offence under these Regulations shall be liable on conviction to a penalty prescribed under the Act . definitions.definition: the Constitution of the United Republic of Tanzania of 1977

the Director of Elections provided for under the Article 74(7) of the Constitution and appointed under section 6 of the Elections Act , 1985 and includes a person for the time being performing any of the functions on his behalf

an election in a ward of an elected councilor and includes a by-election for that purpose. " election officer " includes a public officer, a temporary employee and an employee on contract terms working with the Electoral Authority
the National Electoral Commission established under Article 74 of the Constitution
a political party registered under the Political Parties Act , 1992
a register established under section 15 A of the Act
a, Permanent National Voters' Register compiled and maintained in accordance with section 15B of the Act
a voters registration centre in a ward

voter's registration record compiled in accordance with the provisions of the Act and these Regulations. " Registration Staff " includes the a Registration Officer, an Assistant Registration Officer, a Registration Assistant and a Camera Operator

a division of the jurisdiction of a local government authority, demarcated as such, in pursuance of the provisions of the Act and declared to be a ward for the purpose of election under section 8 of the Act
a card issued under the Act , verifying that a person named in such card has been registered as voter. Part II – Appointment of registration staff
